behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at the parser->hook-handler boundary, not just isolated parsing logic. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network/subprocess required; in-process wiring is sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System because behavior text says only 'parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling' (no real process requirement).
behaviors.test_description: Run an in-process hook input pipeline test that feeds valid JSON with code fences, backticks, and $vars; assert parse succeeds and downstream handler receives the expected structured payload without error traces.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requires parser error output plus trace formatting/sink contract across modules. REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced in-process; no OS primitive needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System because behavior text is about malformed JSON -> trace record content, not process/IO behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Feed malformed JSON into the in-process parse-and-trace path and assert a `json_parse_failed` record is emitted with location/context fields (e.g., snippet/offset) sufficient to identify the malformed segment.

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug appears when parse error is handed to policy and telemetry intent marker, i.e., module handoff. REAL-INFRA: explicitly 'local error-policy path' indicates no real OS/network/subprocess requirement. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System due concrete disqualifier 'local'.
behaviors.test_description: Inject a parse-failure outcome into the local policy flow and assert returned result is handled (no exception propagated) and telemetry-intent flag/field is set as expected.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits the expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: explicit 'real process execution' and malformed stdin handling require subprocess/OS-level behavior. MOCK-HIDE: in-process mocks can miss crash/exit sequencing and signal emission timing. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Integration because behavior text requires real process + stdin + before-exit signal.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn the hook executable as a subprocess, write malformed JSON to stdin, close stdin, and assert non-crash termination plus presence of expected parse-failure trace signal before process exit.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: 'real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink)' implies end-to-end process I/O and actual sink behavior. MOCK-HIDE: mocked stdin/sink can hide serialization/flush/path issues. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Integration because behavior explicitly requires real invocation path.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the hook through its real entrypoint, pass malformed stdin, and assert the trace sink receives/writes the expected `json_parse_failed` artifact with required fields.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requires comparing outcomes across parser + classification/trace behavior for two input classes. REAL-INFRA: no requirement for real OS/network/process in behavior text. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System because distinction can be validated via in-process pipeline with paired fixtures.
behaviors.test_description: Run a paired regression suite in-process: valid markdown-heavy JSON fixtures must succeed with no parse-failure trace; malformed JSON fixtures must emit `json_parse_failed`. Assert no cross-labeling between the two classes.
